def.normie: A new pop song by British singer-songwriter Sienna Spiro from The Devil Wears Prada 2 soundtrack. Creators are using it on fashion hauls, outfit checks, and aspirational shopping clips — anything with that polished, runway energy.
def.normal: Soundtrack drop turned algorithm gift: 'Material Lover' is the must-use audio of mid-June, with the fashion lane jumping first and shopping vlogs close behind. The window is wide open this week.
def.zoomer: Sienna sang one (1) Devil Wears Prada 2 song and now every fit check has the same backing track. Get on it before the brand accounts start scoring their Q2 lookbook to it — three weeks max.

trends.name: "Remember who you are" / Freakin' Out

def.normie: Reels trend pairing 'Freakin' Out' by Dexter and the Moonrocks with the phrase 'remember who you are' over travel, training, and personal-growth montages. Cinematic, emotional, very main-character.
def.normal: Adventure-and-transformation audio of the month. The 'remember who you are' caption locked in last week and Reels is rewarding early users hard; travel and fitness niches got there first, glow-up edits are catching up.
def.zoomer: Stand in a windy field. Say 'remember who you are.' Watch the algorithm love you. The format is so legible it can't NOT cook for at least one more week, then it becomes parody.

trends.origin: Dexter and the Moonrocks 'Freakin' Out' → Reels trend, 2026
trends.vid: remember who you are reels trend

trends.name: Beez in the Trap × What's Up? mashup

def.normie: A mashup blending Nicki Minaj's 'Beez in the Trap' with 4 Non Blondes' 'What's Up?'. The combination of rap menace and emotional rock vocals makes it equal parts funny and dramatic — creators are using it for everything from gym sets to existential breakups.
def.normal: The June rap-meets-rock mashup. Hooks two very different audiences (Nicki stans + millennial alt-rock muscle memory) which is why it travels so well across niches. Still building; should peak next week.
def.zoomer: Two energies that should not fit fitting perfectly. Format works because the drop is funny and the 'what's going on' bridge is unironically emotional. It's gonna get one (1) very bad brand activation and then it dies, but until then, gold.

trends.vid: beez in the trap whats up mashup

trends.name: "no no no NO NO NO"

def.normie: A short audio clip where someone says 'no no no' quietly and then yells 'NO NO NO' as panic sets in.
def.normal: The "I Green Screen Things" sound where escalating "no"s match an escalating crash-out.
def.zoomer: Pure pre-crashout audio. The brilliance is the build: quiet refusal, public denial, then the howl.

trends.origin: I Green Screen Things audio, TikTok 2026

trends.name: "Hate That I Made You Love Me" mashup

def.normie: A new mashup using Ariana Grande's song 'Petal' that creators have been jumping on this week.
def.normal: Mashup audio that started rising after Ariana Grande's Petal dropped. Fast-growing lip-sync trend.
def.zoomer: Petal release, mashup wave, lip-sync season — textbook.

trends.vid: hate that i made you love me trend

trends.name: KitKat Heist memes

def.normie: Memes about the real-life European theft of around 400,000 KitKat bars.
def.normal: Memes about the real ~400,000 KitKat theft in Europe. Peaked late May/early June.
def.zoomer: The cinematic universe is canon. Drifting into reference-meme zone.

trends.origin: Real European logistics theft → meme, mid-2026

trends.name: Nihilistic Penguin

def.normie: Old footage of a penguin walking alone toward the mountains instead of the sea.
def.normal: Werner Herzog's 2007 documentary penguin became 2026's mascot for opting out.
def.zoomer: Herzog filmed him in 2007, we caught up emotionally in 2026.

trends.origin: 'Encounters at the End of the World' (2007) → meme, 2026

def.normie: A format where dramatic saxophone music starts quietly and gets louder as a situation gets worse.
def.normal: The escalation-saxophone format: music swells as the on-screen situation collapses.
def.zoomer: The sax went full crescendo and overtook the timeline.

trends.name: I am my perimeter

def.normie: A streamer's deadpan 'I am my perimeter and my perimeter is me' became a catchphrase.
def.normal: Snapiyya's Rainbow Six Siege line as all-purpose 'locked in' declaration.
def.zoomer: Bro got rushed and achieved enlightenment on stream.

trends.origin: Snapiyya / Rainbow Six Siege stream clip, 2026

def.normie: 'AI slop' is the term for cheap, mass-produced AI content clogging up feeds.
def.normal: Backlash against low-effort AI content is dominating commentary feeds.
def.zoomer: The Slop Wars: phase two. The discourse about slop now outproduces the slop.

def.normie: X rolled out a 'Certified Banger' badge for viral posts and the internet immediately fought about it.
def.normal: X's new viral-post badge launched first week of June and became the discourse.
def.zoomer: The badge is the meme; the absence of the badge is the meme; the cope is the meme.

def.normie: A movement calling for a return to older, simpler human-made internet humour.
def.normal: The push for nostalgic, authentic memes as reaction to AI slop and brain-rot fatigue.
def.zoomer: The Reset is a meme about wanting better memes, which is correctly very us.

def.normie: An insult for robots, AI, or people who rely on AI too much — borrowed from Star Wars.
def.normal: Star Wars' battle-droid slur, repurposed as the internet's everyday anti-AI insult.
def.zoomer: Now in the 'said unironically by your dad in three months' phase.

def.normie: To completely lose your cool — a meltdown, usually public.
def.normal: Losing emotional control spectacularly; 'a crashout' is the episode or the person.

def.normie: A new way to call someone empty or absent — like the '404 page not found' error.
def.normal: The '-coded' suffix found its tech-meme breakout.
def.zoomer: Radar called it, the timeline confirmed it.

def.normie: Fan theory claiming the Stranger Things finale was an illusion.
def.normal: The viral theory that the Stranger Things finale concealed a hidden episode.

def.normie: A mash-up of 'chopped' (unattractive) and 'doppelganger': an unflattering lookalike.
def.zoomer: Word will survive longer than the format that birthed it.

def.normie: The joke that consuming too much silly internet content rots your brain — Oxford's 2024 word of the year.
def.normal: Universally understood, no longer cutting edge.
def.zoomer: Saying you have brain rot is no longer a flex — it's a census statistic.

battles.q: Which audio is winning this week?
Which X feature became its own meme?

hot: Devil Wears Prada 2 soundtrack just colonised fit-check audio
Reels' main-character glow-up audio of the week
Beez in the Trap × What's Up? mashup
rap menace meets alt-rock catharsis, fits everywhere
died: deported by the Great Meme Reset
the irony collapsed in on itself
faded before the algorithm could find it
incoming: the format mocking people upset their post didn't get the badge is forming
tip: This is the rare soundtrack-driven trend with juice in two niches at once — fashion AND general aspirational. Window: 7-10 days before brand accounts arrive.
weird: Our radar flagged this last cycle as 'incoming'. It crossed into general slang within two weeks.

radar.name: "Banger badge cope"
radar.sig: This format is about to explode
radar.det: Posts mocking people whose viral tweets didn't get X's new Certified Banger badge are forming a template.

radar.name: Petal album residue
radar.det: Beyond the 'hate that I made you love me' mashup, more Petal cuts are getting CapCut-templated.

radar.name: PlayStation actual gameplay
radar.sig: Gaming circles are processing a vibe shift
radar.det: Sony's June State of Play broke a multi-year pattern of CGI-only reveals.

radar.name: Heist-core aesthetic
radar.sig: Niche but spreading across food and fashion
radar.det: Post-KitKat, playful 'heist' aesthetics are bubbling in food and fashion content.

radar.name: Clanker court
radar.sig: AI-on-trial skits are becoming a genre
radar.det: Mock-trial videos putting AI tools and bot accounts on trial are building.

radar.name: Analog posting
radar.sig: The Reset's aesthetic wing is gaining ground
radar.det: Film photos, unedited clips, deliberately low-tech content as anti-slop signalling.

radar.name: "-coded" suffix wave
radar.det: After 404 coded broke through, error-coded, beta-coded, captcha-coded are incubating.

radar.name: Heatwave content
radar.sig: Weather is about to drive a content wave
radar.det: European heatwave coverage is starting to spawn the familiar summer-discourse cycle.
